newsSinceJun6: No fresh corporate news since June 6, but a structural fact invalidates the listing: a CVC Capital / Risto Siilasmaa consortium (Diana BidCo Oy) acquired WithSecure at EUR 1.70/share; the tender offer completed ~10 Nov 2025 with a 93.1% stake, the company applied for delisting from Nasdaq Helsinki, and compulsory squeeze-out of remaining shares was expected during H1 2026. June 8/9 quotes are null, consistent with the share no longer trading as a public equity.

sources.label: CVC-led consortium secures 93.1% stake in WithSecure and completes tender offer (M&A Insights)
WithSecure applies for delisting of shares from Nasdaq Helsinki (MarketScreener)
Inside information: CVC/Siilasmaa consortium recommended cash tender offer of EUR 1.70 per share via Diana BidCo Oy (Investegate)
WithSecure Oyj (WITH.HE) quote page - no live price (Yahoo Finance)

verdicts.rankRationale: WITH.HE is no longer an investable alpha vehicle on the scaling list: the CVC/Siilasmaa take-private completed (93.1% tendered Nov 2025), delisting from Nasdaq Helsinki was applied for, and H1 2026 squeeze-out caps any minority holder at EUR 1.70 cash. Yesterday's hold relied on stale market-cap data; null Jun 8/9 quotes confirm the public listing is effectively dead, so it should fall to the bottom of the page pending removal.
verdicts.bull: Any residual minority shares are redeemed at EUR 1.70 in the squeeze-out, so downside is essentially nil from here; the MDR/cyber-resilience franchise itself lives on under CVC ownership.
verdicts.neutral: The managed-detection thesis was never disproven, but it now accrues to private-equity owners; for a public-markets watchlist the position is a cash-like redemption claim with zero thesis optionality.
verdicts.bear: Holding a delisted, squeeze-out-pending Finnish small cap means no liquidity, no upside participation, and dead capital until redemption settles; keeping it ranked misstates the watchlist's investable universe.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Completion of the Finnish Companies Act compulsory redemption and formal delisting settlement (expected H1 2026), which removes the ticker entirely.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Process risk only: redemption-price arbitration or settlement delays leave minority cash tied up longer, with no offsetting upside.
